name: Fletcher Henderson
description: American pianist, bandleader, arranger and composer (1897–1952)
abstract: James Fletcher Hamilton Henderson (December 18, 1897 – December 29, 1952) was an American pianist, bandleader, arranger and composer, important in the development of big band jazz and swing music. He was one of the most prolific black musical arrangers and, along with Duke Ellington, is considered one of the most influential arrangers an…

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: I had -- I never could remember whether this was before -- well, when I was about 14 or 15 years old I discovered Fletcher Henderson's band... I went to this dance and I heard Fletcher Henderson's band

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Barrett
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Fletcher Henderson's arrangements and his work with Louis Armstrong in the early 20s, but never claims to have met, played with, or worked directly with Henderson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Barrett interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Norris Turney
about_quotes.evidence: And we used to listen to big bands, Fletcher Henderson, Earl Hines, Basie when he first went to New York, and all the big bands you know, Jimmie Lunceford.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I began to listen to Ellington records and Basie records and Erskine Hawkins and Fletcher Henderson and all the good jazz bands of that day.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: people like Benny Carter, Duke Ellington, Art Tatum, I mean Fats Waller, Fletcher Henderson, Bix Beiderbecke.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I remember years ago seeing Fletcher Henderson's band with Coleman Hawkins in the band...
